situational_context: Early rebuild phase, Week 1 of structured training reintroduction. First Zwift session (Climb On Lite, load 15) added alongside e-bike commuting. Chronic load at 18.8 (1.2% of 2023 peak). HRV showed intra-week decline from 42 to 32, attributed to regression from transient high plus first multi-session training day, not illness. RHR stable (avg 52.9) with one-day spike to 56 on Apr 18 coinciding with body battery nadir (28). Cohabitant disruption: night of Apr 18-19 heavily fragmented (5 wakings in first 2.5h). Sleep adequate most nights (7.22h avg) but variable (5.95-8.29h). Weight down ~1 kg to 105.85 (only 2 data points). Protein intake consistently below target (avg 119g vs 142g). Readiness consistently high — autonomic capacity for load increase confirmed. VO2max stable: running 40.9, cycling 39.

recommendations.text: Complete all 3 scheduled Zwift sessions this week — consistency is the rate-limiting factor for rebuild at this stage
Hit protein target (≥140g/day) on all days, especially training days — current deficit (avg 119g) will limit adaptation as load increases
recommendations.measurable: ≥5 days with protein ≥140g logged in OH

recommendations.text: Discuss recent weight trend and general fatigue patterns at Wednesday GP appointment — opportunity to mention interest in routine bloodwork given rebuild phase
recommendations.measurable: GP visit completed, any ordered labs noted

markers_to_watch.threshold: Sustaining >35 ms confirms recovery capacity; drop below 32 for 3+ days warrants deload
markers_to_watch.reason: Late-week dip to 32-34 needs monitoring to confirm it was transient
Persistent protein deficit at 84% of target will become rate-limiting as training load increases

markers_to_watch.threshold: Target gradual increase toward 25-30 over next 2 weeks
markers_to_watch.reason: Currently at 1.2% of historical peak — structured sessions should begin moving this
